behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure boundary is not just local parsing; it is whether the real STT model preserves diarization labels in realistic audio. REAL-INFRA exists (external API), but LLM-DEP applies first because correctness depends on real model output content. MULTI-STEP is not required to validate this behavior: we can assert speaker labels directly on STT output before summarization. Rejection-gate: Workflow was considered and rejected because the behavior text is specifically about STT output preservation, not a required sequence of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the actual speech-to-text API with speaker diarization enabled, then assert transcript segments contain stable speaker labels mapped to distinct attendees; include overlapping speech and turn-taking samples to catch diarization loss.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is local parsing/normalization logic and can fail within one function/object boundary using transcript text fixtures. No required OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA no), no dependence on real AI output quality (LLM-DEP no), and no multi-step agent chain (MULTI-STEP no). Rejection-gate: Integration was considered and rejected because behavior text focuses on extraction logic itself, not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript snippets containing explicit assignments (e.g., 'Dana will send the draft by Friday') and assert structured output contains exact assignee and task text fields with correct token boundaries.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real summarization model chooses to include for each assignee; deterministic stubs can hide omission failures (LLM-DEP yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). REAL-INFRA may exist but is not the key minimum boundary. Rejection-gate: Workflow was considered and rejected because this behavior can be validated with one real summarization step given a prepared transcript/assignment set.
behaviors.test_description: Submit transcripts with known attendee-task assignments to the real summarization LLM and assert output action_items covers every assigned attendee at least once; run multiple times to detect omission variability.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The stated failure is module handoff/routing between decision-identification output and schema assembly, not single-line algorithm only. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed, (b) no dependence on real AI output if identified decisions are fixture inputs, (c) no multi-agentic chain required, (d) this is local orchestration/state handoff across modules. Rejection-gate: Agentic was considered and rejected because behavior text says 'identified decisions' (already identified), so this test can isolate routing without model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire decision identifier output module to summary schema builder in-process; pass fixtures containing identified decisions plus narrative text and assert all decisions land in `decisions` schema fields and are absent from generic narrative slots.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality/placement behavior of the real summarization model (or model-driven extractor). A stubbed model would always pass formatting expectations and hide true misses (LLM-DEP yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejection-gate: Workflow was considered and rejected because one real model decision step from transcript to summary can expose the failure without requiring chained agent stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use transcripts with clearly stated decisions and run real summarization; assert those decisions appear in `decisions` section with semantic match checks and are not only present in freeform narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is a sequence: chunking -> repeated STT/LLM processing -> merge coherence across segments. MULTI-STEP yes by definition, and each segment processing depends on real model behavior, so lower levels can miss cross-step degradation. Rejection-gate: Agentic was considered and rejected because one model call is insufficient; behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing and merged coherence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation settings, verify sequential segment processing order, then evaluate merged final summary for cross-segment consistency (no dropped late-segment tasks/decisions, no contradictory attendee attribution).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end pipeline behavior across multiple stages including real transcription and summarization model calls; MULTI-STEP and LLM-DEP are both intrinsic. REAL-INFRA is also present (upload/delivery), but Workflow is the minimal boundary that captures full autonomous completion failures. Rejection-gate: System was considered and rejected because real infrastructure alone does not cover multi-agentic-stage correctness and sequencing required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like E2E run with a real meeting recording: upload via real entrypoint, process through real STT and summarization, and assert final structured payload is delivered successfully with required sections and no manual retries/intervention.
